@@ -8,7 +8,7 @@
 {"id":"frankentorch-2er","title":"Add custom autograd Function API (torch.autograd.Function equivalent)","description":"Users cannot currently define custom backward functions. This is arguably the single most impactful missing feature for real-world use — without it, any layer whose backward isn't built into FrankenTorch's op set is impossible to implement.

PyTorch's torch.autograd.Function API allows users to:
1. Define a custom forward() that registers itself on the autograd tape
2. Define a custom backward() that computes gradients for the custom op
3. Save tensors for backward via ctx.save_for_backward()

Implementation plan:
1. Add a CustomFunction trait in ft-autograd with forward/backward methods
2. Add a TensorNodeOp::Custom variant to the tensor tape
3. Add session.apply_custom_function() to ft-api that invokes the user's forward, registers the op on the tape, and stores the backward closure
4. During backward traversal, call the user's backward function when encountering Custom nodes
5. Handle save_for_backward by capturing TensorNodeIds in the closure context

Key design decisions:
- The backward closure must receive upstream gradient and return downstream gradients
- Must respect no_grad context (custom forward inside no_grad should not record on tape)
- Must work with the existing deterministic backward scheduler
- Error handling: user backward functions should return Result, not panic

Tests:
- Unit test: custom ReLU equivalent (forward: max(0,x), backward: 1 if x>0 else 0)
- Unit test: custom function with multiple inputs/outputs
- Unit test: custom function inside no_grad context (should not record)
- Unit test: custom function backward produces correct gradients (numerical gradient check)
- E2E test: train a model with a custom layer and verify loss decreases
- Edge case: nested custom functions
- Edge case: custom function that saves tensors for backward","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:46.006446323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:15:34.162735830Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:15:34.162441333Z","close_reason":"Core functionality already exists: tensor_apply_function() with FunctionCtx (save_for_backward, needs_input_grad) + backward closure. Extensive tests in ft-autograd (lines 17484+) and used internally by ft-api for ~12 ops. The closure-based API is the Rust-idiomatic equivalent of PyTorch's class-based torch.autograd.Function. A trait wrapper could be added later for API sugar but is not blocking.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-2os","title":"Implement slogdet, pinverse, kthvalue, linalg_cross, linalg_vector_norm","description":"Add numerically stable linalg and selection ops: slogdet (sign+log determinant), pinverse (SVD-based pseudoinverse), kthvalue (k-th smallest), linalg_cross (3D cross product), linalg_vector_norm (flexible vector norm). Used in scientific computing, regularization, and model analysis.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-28T14:15:54.437563761Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-29T02:09:46.045687331Z","closed_at":"2026-03-29T02:09:46.045431557Z","close_reason":"slogdet/cross already existed. Implemented pinverse (SVD-based), kthvalue (with index), linalg_vector_norm (L0/L1/L2/Linf). 9 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-35e","title":"Update FEATURE_PARITY.md to honestly track unimplemented PyTorch subsystems","description":"FEATURE_PARITY.md currently marks 'Full PyTorch drop-in surface: parity_green' but omits major PyTorch subsystems from the tracking matrix entirely, creating a misleading picture.

What to add:
1. New section: 'Deferred Subsystems (not yet in scope)' with explicit not_started status for: GPU/CUDA backend, Distributed training (DDP/FSDP), JIT/TorchScript/torch.compile, Quantization, Sparse tensors, Mixed precision (AMP), ONNX export, Gradient checkpointing, Custom autograd Functions (torch.autograd.Function).
2. For each: document why it's deferred (e.g., 'CPU-first vertical slice, GPU planned for Phase 3+'), link to any future closure beads.
3. Change 'Full PyTorch drop-in surface: parity_green' to accurately reflect that it covers the CPU eager-mode subset only.
4. Add a 'Parity Scope Declaration' section that explicitly states what IS and IS NOT in scope for the current phase.

This is the single most important documentation fix — without it, the project appears done when it's at ~70% of PyTorch's core API surface.

Tests: N/A (documentation only). Verify by reading the updated file and confirming every major PyTorch subsystem is either tracked as green or explicitly listed as deferred.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:28.495902714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:04:43.553923709Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:04:43.553642487Z","close_reason":"Already completed by another agent: Parity Scope Declaration section added, Full PyTorch drop-in changed to parity_gap, Deferred Subsystems section with 9 entries and tracking beads, Gap Policy updated. Verified all referenced beads exist and tensor_apply_function claim is accurate.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"frankentorch-3cp","title":"Capture committed perf-SLO baseline artifact for G5","description":"Follow-up to frankentorch-vws.\n\nThe perf-SLO runner/gate now exists in ft-conformance, strict cargo gates pass, and we captured a verified single-sample full-profile measurement in artifacts/phase2c/performance/perf_slo_report_full_once.json plus PERF_SLO_SUMMARY_FULL_ONCE.md. The current result shows all five direct spec budgets failing and the three regression budgets remaining baseline_required without a committed baseline.\n\nRemaining work:\n1. Run the full perf-SLO profile with the intended statistical envelope (default 100 iterations, warmups as configured) on a suitable worker/host.\n2. Commit a stable baseline artifact at the canonical perf_slo baseline path for regression comparisons.\n3. Decide whether the single-sample full_once artifact should stay as an exploratory snapshot or be superseded by the committed baseline bundle.\n4. Hand the baseline path/contract to frankentorch-ioo so G5 can call run_perf_slos + check_perf_slos deterministically in CI.\n\nNotes:\n- rch exec validates runtime execution but does not automatically persist generated artifacts back into the local worktree across separate invocations, so baseline capture should use a workflow that intentionally preserves the produced artifact bundle.\n- Direct spec-budget failures from the single-sample full profile are already documented in artifacts/phase2c/performance/PERF_SLO_SUMMARY_FULL_ONCE.md.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T20:53:37.780725984Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:53:37.780725984Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankentorch-myj","title":"Fail closed on invalid functional dropout probabilities and embedding indices","description":"ft-api functional helpers currently accept invalid inputs too permissively: functional_dropout does not reject p<0 or p>1, and functional_embedding lossy-casts float indices to usize instead of rejecting negative or fractional values. Tighten these APIs to explicit fail-closed behavior and add targeted tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-28T14:15:30.628000763Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-28T14:19:39.422034110Z","closed_at":"2026-03-28T14:19:39.421762196Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-oaw","title":"Stabilize rch remote sync/artifact retrieval under volatile workspace churn","notes":"Investigated repo-local rch causes. Findings: no project .rch/config.toml, no .cargo/config.toml, no legacy_pytorch_code tree in this checkout, no local .venv*, and no FrankenTorch-specific target-dir override. Global rch config already excludes target/, target_*/, target-*/, and .beads/. Remaining intermittent rsync/file-vanished noise appears daemon/artifact-sync side rather than a safe repo-local misconfiguration to edit here.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T05:33:36.582627630Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T05:38:47.775805569Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T05:38:47.775198931Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-ofr","title":"Implement RMSNorm in ft-nn","description":"Add RMSNorm normalization layer. Critical for modern LLM architectures (LLaMA, Mistral, DeepSeek). Simpler than LayerNorm: normalize by RMS without centering. Formula: x * rsqrt(mean(x^2) + eps) * weight","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-27T17:33:20.154495928Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T17:36:24.809827674Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T17:36:24.809579524Z","close_reason":"Implemented RMSNorm with batched forward, weight parameter, shape validation. 4 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-olf","title":"Replace placeholder perf-SLO baseline with higher-iteration bundle","description":"Follow-up to frankentorch-3cp.

The canonical baseline path for G5 is now artifacts/phase2c/performance/perf_slo_baseline_v1.json, but it is intentionally a single-sample full-profile anchor captured on 2026-04-08 UTC.

Remaining work:
1. Run the full perf-SLO profile with a stronger statistical envelope, targeting the intended 100 iterations plus warmup.
2. Preserve the generated artifact bundle directly instead of reconstructing it from stdout.
3. Replace the placeholder baseline and baseline summary with the stronger bundle, or version it explicitly if both need to coexist.
4. Re-assess regression-budget stability after the stronger baseline is committed.

This is quality-improvement work, not a prerequisite for the newly normalized baseline path contract itself.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:27:16.452336177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-08T03:28:49.480764447Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-pku","title":"Implement tril, triu, tril_indices, triu_indices","description":"Add triangular matrix operations: tril (lower triangle), triu (upper triangle), tril_indices, triu_indices. Critical for attention masks in transformers.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:14:34.911778975Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T18:18:56.492931488Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T18:18:56.492684951Z","close_reason":"tril and triu already existed. Added tril_indices and triu_indices with offset support. 4 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-plj","title":"Add gradient checkpointing (torch.utils.checkpoint equivalent)","description":"torch.utils.checkpoint.checkpoint() allows trading compute for memory by not saving intermediate activations during forward, then recomputing them during backward. This is essential for training large models that don't fit in memory.

Implementation plan:
1. Add a session.checkpoint(function, inputs) method that:
   a. Runs the function in no_grad context (saves no intermediates)
   b. Records a special CheckpointedSegment node on the autograd tape
   c. During backward: re-runs the function WITH grad enabled to recompute activations, then computes gradients normally
2. The key invariant: gradients must be identical whether checkpoint is used or not (just slower with lower memory)

Design considerations:
- Must work with the existing TensorTape backward scheduler
- Must properly handle nested checkpointing
- Must respect the DAC contract (deterministic replay must still work)
- Re-execution during backward must produce identical activations (deterministic PRNG state needs saving)

Tests:
- Unit test: simple function with checkpoint vs without — gradients must match exactly
- Unit test: nested checkpointing (checkpoint inside checkpoint)
- Unit test: checkpoint respects manual_seed for reproducibility
- E2E test: train a deep network with and without checkpointing — final loss should be identical
- Memory test: verify peak memory is lower with checkpointing (if measurable)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:17:52.757885399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:36:14.882158581Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:36:14.881802397Z","close_reason":"Implemented session.checkpoint() that trades compute for memory. Runs function in no_grad (no intermediates saved), registers backward node via tensor_apply_function that re-executes the function with grad enabled during backward. 3 tests: correct forward output, gradient equivalence with non-checkpointed, multiple inputs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-ps7","title":"Implement tensor_vander (Vandermonde matrix)","description":"Add torch.vander for polynomial fitting and signal processing.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:14:34.983231294Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T18:45:47.810840473Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T18:45:47.810526477Z","close_reason":"Implemented tensor_vander with increasing/decreasing modes and custom N. 3 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
